name: Via de la Plata — circular from Almadén de la Plata
name_original: Vía de la Plata — circular desde Almadén de la Plata
region_intro: Jogging around Almadén De La Plata offers diverse routes within the Sierra Norte Natural Park, characterized by a mid-mountain landscape. The region features extensive dehesas, forests of holm oaks, cork oaks, and chestnut trees, alongside river forests along the Cala and Viar rivers. Trails often traverse Mediterranean scrub and lead to geological formations like the Berrocal Granite, providing varied terrain for running. Best jogging routes around Almadén De La Plata The most popular jogging route is Via de la Plata — circular from Almadén de la Plata, a 11.0 miles (17.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 6 minutes to complete, following a historical section of the Way of Saint James. Another top favourite among local runners is Sierra Morena Trail (GR 48): El Real de la Jara Loop, a moderate 8.5 miles (13.8 km) path winding through the dehesa landscape of the Sierra Norte Natural Park. Local runners also love the Cerro del Calvario Viewpoint loop from La Polana, a 7.7 miles (12.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain to a viewpoint, often completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes. Jogging around Almadén De La Plata is defined by dehesa landscapes, river forests, and Mediterranean scrub, with routes offering options for different ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 10 runners have used komoot to explore Almadén De La Plata's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails around Almadén de la Plata?
A: The running trails in Almadén de la Plata offer a diverse mid-mountain landscape. You'll encounter extensive &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; (wooded pastures), forests of holm oaks, cork oaks, and chestnut trees, as well as Mediterranean scrub. Many routes also feature lush river forests along the banks of the Cala and Viar rivers, and some even pass by unique geological formations like the Berrocal Granite.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Almadén de la Plata?
A: While the guide primarily features moderate to difficult routes, the Sierra Norte Natural Park, where Almadén de la Plata is located, offers a network of trails with varying difficulty levels, including some of low to medium difficulty. These paths often traverse gentler dehesa landscapes or follow riverbanks, providing good options for those new to running or looking for an easier pace.

Q: How many running routes are available in the Almadén de la Plata area?
A: This guide highlights 5 top running routes around Almadén de la Plata, showcasing the best of the region's landscape. However, the area, being entirely within the Sierra Norte Natural Park, boasts an extensive network of trails, offering many more opportunities for exploration beyond these featured routes.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Almadén de la Plata?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013151112/via-de-la-plata-circular-from-almaden-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via de la Plata — circular from Almadén de la Plata&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 11-mile loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013653705/sierra-morena-trail-gr-48-el-real-de-la-jara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Morena Trail (GR 48): El Real de la Jara Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is an 8.5-mile moderate path.

Q: Can I see waterfalls while jogging in Almadén de la Plata?
A: Absolutely! The area is home to beautiful waterfalls that can be incorporated into your run. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chorro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, located in a narrow rocky gorge, is a notable highlight. You might also encounter other cascades along the Arroyo de Las Calzadillas and Las Hoyas, providing scenic spots and the soothing sound of flowing water.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the running trails?
A: Yes, Almadén de la Plata offers a rich historical context. You can run sections of the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013151112/via-de-la-plata-circular-from-almaden-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata&lt;/a&gt;, part of the Way of Saint James. Many trails also pass by fascinating archaeological remains, such as the Bronze Age Necropolis at La Traviesa and the Roman quarries of Los Covachos, which include an impressive cave.

Q: Where can I find panoramic views on the running trails?
A: Several trails lead to stunning viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38166994" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Calvario Viewpoint loop from La Polana&lt;/a&gt; is a great option, leading to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/650365" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Calvario Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; itself, which offers breathtaking vistas of the surrounding Sierra. Other viewpoints like La Bordalla also provide expansive views of the natural park.

Q: Are there any routes that follow rivers or offer riverside scenery?
A: Yes, the banks of the Cala and Viar rivers provide pleasant and often flatter terrains for running. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rivera de Cala&lt;/a&gt; is a beautiful spot, and routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167031" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo del Real de la Jara – Cala Riverside loop from El Real de la Jara&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlight riverside paths, offering lush, riparian scenery.

Q: Are the running routes in Almadén de la Plata family-friendly?
A: Many trails within the Sierra Norte Natural Park are suitable for families, especially those of low to medium difficulty. Look for routes that are shorter in distance and have less elevation gain, often found along riverbanks or through the dehesa, to ensure a comfortable experience for all family members.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Almadén de la Plata?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails within the Sierra Norte Natural Park. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to check for any specific local regulations or protected zones within the park where restrictions might apply.

Q: Is there parking available near the running trailheads in Almadén de la Plata?
A: Yes, Almadén de la Plata is accustomed to outdoor enthusiasts. You can typically find parking within the town itself, and many trailheads or popular starting points for routes will have designated parking areas. It's recommended to check specific route details for the most convenient parking spots.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Almadén de la Plata?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for running in Almadén de la Plata, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summers can be very hot, so early mornings or late evenings are recommended if running during this season. Winters are mild, making it possible to run year-round, though some trails might be muddy after rain.

Q: How can I reach Almadén de la Plata using public transport?
A: Almadén de la Plata is accessible by public transport, primarily via bus services connecting it to Seville and other nearby towns. For detailed schedules and routes, it's best to consult local transport providers. Once in Almadén, many trailheads are within walking distance or a short local transfer.

Q: What makes running in Almadén de la Plata unique?
A: Running in Almadén de la Plata offers a unique blend of natural beauty and historical depth. You'll experience the distinctive dehesa landscape, ancient cork oak forests, and the tranquility of the Sierra Norte Natural Park, all while potentially tracing sections of the historic Vía de la Plata or discovering archaeological sites. It's an immersive experience that combines physical activity with cultural and natural exploration.
tips:
The Mirador Cerro del Calvario is a beautiful observation point located in the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park, near Almadén de la Plata. This viewpoint offers spectacular views of the region and is a popular hiking spot.
The route to the viewpoint is moderate and accessible for most fitness levels. Also near the viewpoint is El Chorro Waterfall, another natural attraction worth visiting.

El Chorro is a narrow gorge through which the Calzadilla stream falls in small and beautiful cascades. Except for its final stretch, it is largely linear, so we will have to follow the same path back and forth.
